      • getArg1Float(co: CachedOps; i: int): float64
        getArg1Int
      • getArg1Int(co: CachedOps; i: int): int

We are implementing a stack-based Virtual Machine to execute bytecode generated by the VanCode compiler. The VM is designed to be simple and portable, easily embeddable in other Nim applications or projects that want to build DSL (domain-specific language) features on top of the VanCode framework.

Imports

chunk, value

Types

CachedOps = ref object
  opcodes*: seq[Opcode]
  arg1*: seq[int64]
  arg2*: seq[int64]
  flags*: seq[int16]
  byteOffsets*: seq[int]
  jumpTargets*: seq[int]
CallFrame = tuple[currentChunk: Chunk, cachedOps: CachedOps, pcIdx: int,
                  stackBottom: int, script: Script]
Represents a call frame for function/procedure calls. 
Coroutine {.acyclic.} = ref object
  state*: CoroutineState
  callee*: Proc
  script*: Script
  savedStack*: seq[Value]
  savedCallStack*: seq[CallFrame]
  savedPcIdx*: int
  savedChunk*: Chunk
  savedCachedOps*: CachedOps
  savedStackBottom*: int
  result*: Value
CoroutineResult = object
  case kind*: CoroutineResultKind
  of crYielded:
    yieldedValue*: Value
  of crCompleted:
    resultValue*: Value
  of crErrored:
    errorMsg*: string
CoroutineResultKind = enum
  crYielded, crCompleted, crErrored
CoroutineState = enum
  csCreated, csSuspended, csRunning, csCompleted, csErrored
Vm {.acyclic.} = ref object
  hotCounts*: Table[string, int]
  hotProcCounts*: Table[string, int]
  importedModules*: Table[string, Script]
  activeCoroutine*: Coroutine
  jit*: JitHooks
The main VM object that holds the execution state and caches 
VMPreferences = object
  enableHotCodeDetection*: bool
  hotProcThreshold*: int = 10
  hotChunkThreshold*: int = 100
Preferences for configuring the VM's behavior. These can be set when creating a new VM instance

Consts

VMInitialPreallocatedStackSize {.intdefine.} = 64
initial size of the VM's evaluation stack. this is just an optimization to avoid frequent resizing during execution of small chunks. it can grow dynamically if needed. 
VMPreallocatedStackSize {.intdefine.} = 16
number of stack slots to preallocate when growing the stack. this is just an optimization to avoid frequent resizing during execution of small chunks. it can grow dynamically if needed.

Procs

proc getCachedOps(vm: Vm; currentChunk: Chunk): CachedOps {....raises: [KeyError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc getHotChunkCount(vm: Vm; chunk: Chunk): int {....raises: [ValueError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc getHotProcCount(vm: Vm; procName: string): int {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
proc interpret(vm: Vm; script: Script; startChunk: Chunk;
               staticString: Option[string] = none(string);
               globaldata = newJObject(); localData = newJObject();
               stepping: Coroutine = nil): Value {.discardable,
    ...raises: [KeyError, ValueError, Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Interpret the given chunk in the context of the provided script. If stepping is provided, the VM runs the coroutine from its saved state until it yields or completes, then returns the yielded/completed value. 
proc newVirtualMachine(prefs: VMPreferences): Vm {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Create a new VM instance with optional preferences. If preferences are not provided, defaults will be used. 
proc newVm(): Vm {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc prewarmScriptOps(vm: Vm; s: Script) {....raises: [KeyError], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Call this once after loading from persistent cache (or after compile) to prewarm the VM's opcode cache with all chunks in the script and its imports 
proc stepCoroutine(vm: Vm; coro: Coroutine): CoroutineResult {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, ValueError, Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}

Templates

template getArg1Float(co: CachedOps; i: int): float64
template getArg1Int(co: CachedOps; i: int): int
